title: pittosporum confertiflorum
intro: Pittosporum confertiflorum, also known as the New Zealand tobacco tree, is a plant species native to New Zealand and Australia. The leaves and flowers of the Pittosporum confertiflorum plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with the leaves said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as being used to treat a range of health issues including fever and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Pittosporum confertiflorum are also believed to provide various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum confertiflorum include infusions made from the leaves and flowers, which can be used as teas or applied topically to the skin.
